description: Cross-implementation canonicalisation vectors for CHAP. Every conforming implementation MUST produce the exact byte string in `canonical` for each `accept` case, and MUST raise an error for each `reject` case. This is what guarantees that a hash or signature computed by one implementation verifies against another. See canonical.ts / canonical.py and the number-format note in the specification.

notes.2.0: A JSON `2.0` parses to the value 2 (integer-valued) and canonicalises to "2" on both sides. Because it is expressed as a JSON literal here, it is covered by the implementation unit tests rather than this file (JSON has no way to distinguish 2 from 2.0).
notes.reject_via_json: The reject cases carry a raw JSON string because some values (e.g. 9007199254740993) cannot be represented exactly once parsed into a double; each implementation parses the JSON, then attempts to canonicalise, and must raise.
